channel.title: RSS Aggregator Feed
channel.description: An aggregated feed of Technology-related news

item.title: Meta's rogue AI agent passed every identity check — four gaps in enterprise IAM explain why
item.description: A rogue AI agent at Meta took action without approval and exposed sensitive company and user data to employees who were not authorized to access it. Meta confirmed the incident to The Information on March 18 but said no user data was ultimately mishandled. The exposure still triggered a major security alert internally.The available evidence suggests the failure occurred after authentication, not during it. The agent held valid credentials, operated inside authorized boundaries, passing every identity check.Summer Yue, director of alignment at Meta Superintelligence Labs, described a different ...

item.title: Bluesky announces $100M Series B after CEO transition
item.description: Bluesky's Series B closed last April but is being shared now, the week after its CEO stepped down.

item.title: Apple TV fans will get a great free upgrade for discovering movies and TV shows soon, but there's a catch
item.description: Apple TV is changing the way it recommends movies and shows to you — say hello to 'Genius Browse'.

item.title: iPhone Air at 6 months — here’s what I love, what I hate, and why it’s ‘the most conflicted I’ve ever been about a phone’
item.description: Apple's iPhone Air has been out for six months — here's everything I've come to love and hate about the device in that time.

item.title: Android will hide app sideloading behind a new one-time security process
item.description: Google has detailed how users will be able to sideload apps from unverified developers once it implements its more restrictive policy towards downloading software on Android. The company originally planned to require all developers to be "verified" to distribute on Android, but softened its stance in November 2025 to allow carveouts for Android power-users and hobbyist developers.For the average Android users, the ability to sideload apps will now be locked behind a multi-step one-time process. Users will first have to enable developer mode in settings, confirm they're not being coached into d...

item.title: Installer des apps Android en dehors du Play Store va devenir beaucoup plus long et compliqué
item.description: La grande différence entre Android et iOS, c'est que le premier permet depuis toujours d'installer des applications en dehors de la boutique officielle. Une souplesse qui se paie par des problèmes de sécurité. Google n'a cessé de poser des barrières aux utilisateurs désirant marcher en dehors des clous, mais le nouveau dispositif se montre particulièrement contraignant....

item.title: The Steam Spring Sale is here with discounts on Arc Raiders, Hades 2 and much more
item.description: The Steam Spring Sale is underway and as usual, there's plenty of good stuff to add to your library. The seasonal discounts will run through Thursday, March 26 at 1PM ET. If there's anything you've been waiting to buy, it's worth checking to see if it's on sale now, because huge chunks of the Steam catalog are at least a little bit off. Recent releases don't usually receive big price cuts during Steam sales, but you can save at least a couple bucks on several 2025 hits this time. The excellent Arc Raiders is $32, Doom: The Dark Ages is about $23 and Battlefield 6 is $42. Silent Hill f is half ...

item.title: Mini PC vendors jump on the OpenClaw bandwagon as Minisforum’s M2 Pro arrives with Intel’s Core Ultra X9 388H CPU and 96GB of RAM — but it won’t be cheap
item.description: Minisforum M2 Pro offers powerful local AI processing with hybrid mode, balancing privacy, cloud use, and addressing concerns over OpenClaw vulnerabilities.

item.title: Python Blood Could Kickstart the Next Big Thing in Weight Loss
item.description: Researchers have found a metabolite in Burmese pythons that suppresses appetite in mice without some of GLP-1's side effects. And humans make it, too.

item.title: Meta will move away from human content moderators in favor of more AI
item.description: A little more than a year after ditching third-party fact checkers and rolling back much of its proactive content moderation, the company says it will further "transform" its approach by drastically reducing the number of human moderators in favor of AI-based systems. The company says the change will happen "over the next few years," and will allow the company to catch more issues faster than its current approach. Meta didn't say how much of its contract workforce might be cut as it makes this transition. The company employs thousands of contractors around the world to review content flagged b...

item.title: Figma takes a hit as Google doubles down on ‘vibe design’
item.description: Google Labs announced new features for its vibe design tool Stitch, as competition in the interface design tool space grows. This week, Google announced new features for its AI-powered interface tool Stitch—in the process, it signaled that it’s going all-in on “vibe design.”

item.title: Geothermal startup Fervo catapults itself over the ‘valley of death’
item.description: Fervo landed a large new loan with terms that strongly suggest the company's most turbulent days are behind it.

item.title: Revisiting ‘The Other’, the Comics Story That May Unlock the Secrets to ‘Spider-Man: Brand New Day’
item.description: This week's first look at the new Spider-Man movie drops a lot of hints that it's taking some influences from a time of change for Peter Parker in the comics.

item.title: Don’t be surprised that the FBI is buying your location data
item.description: The FBI has confirmed to the Senate it is once again buying data which can be used to track the locations of US citizens. That may have surprised the people who thought the precedent in Carpenter v. United States prohibited it. But while that case examined if it was legal for law enforcement to obtain location data from mobile networks without a warrant, here the FBI and other agencies have found a way to skirt the Fourth Amendment entirely. Over the last few years, they have taken to just buying location data from the same companies which power the enormous online advertising ecosystem. Where...

item.title: A rogue AI led to a serious security incident at Meta
item.description: For almost two hours last week, Meta employees had unauthorized access to company and user data thanks to an AI agent that gave an employee inaccurate technical advice, as previously reported by The Information. Meta spokesperson Tracy Clayton said in a statement to The Verge that "no user data was mishandled" during the incident. A […]

item.title: Microsoft’s go-to Xbox controller is selling at its best price of the year
item.description: Microsoft’s Xbox console strategy needs work, but its Bluetooth-ready controller that shipped alongside the Xbox Series X and Series S is fantastic. We think it’s one of the best Xbox controllers out there, thanks to its comfortable design, multiplatform compatibility (it works great on the Steam Deck and PC, too), and its textured grips and […]

item.title: This is the most overlooked hour in a solopreneur’s week
item.description: Why we need dedicated time for admin work. I’m obsessive about my to-do lists. Everything I need to get done goes on my list so I don’t lose sight of it.

item.title: FCC Enforcement Chief Offered to Help Brendan Carr Target Disney, Records Show
item.description: Last year, as FCC chair Brendan Carr threatened ABC over a Jimmy Kimmel monolog, a civil servant overseeing West Coast stations privately pledged support, according to emails obtained by WIRED.

item.title: New Research Challenges One of Archaeology’s Most Important Discoveries
item.description: Monte Verde, the 'oldest site in the Americas,' may not be as old as believed.

item.title: Google Shakes Up Its Browser Agent Team Amid OpenClaw Craze
item.description: As Silicon Valley obsesses over a new wave of AI coding agents, Google and other AI labs are shifting their bets.

item.title: Google signs data center deal which includes a 20-year commitment to add new clean power
item.description: Google's prospective Michigan-based data center could give the Detroit area access to clean energy.

item.title: All the wrong EVs are getting canceled
item.description: These past few weeks have been particularly brutal for the EV industry - and anyone who believes that electric vehicles are the future. Thanks to slowing demand and policy whiplashes, automakers are on an EV murder spree, killing a host of promising new models. The EV graveyard grows bigger by the minute. And unfortunately, as […]

item.title: The Original AirPods Max Are $100 Off
item.description: Save big on Apple’s previous-generation premium over-ear headset.

item.title: Feeling the heat? I tried the Shark ChillPill — a portable fan with misting spray for instant relief — and I'm not going anywhere without it this summer
item.description: The ChillPill is small enough to keep in your bag, and has three different cooling attachments for instant relief from summer stickiness.

item.title: Meta Will Keep Horizon Worlds Alive in VR ‘for the Foreseeable Future’
item.description: A day after saying it would shut down its metaverse, Meta CTO Andrew Bosworth announced the service will remain available in VR—with limited support.

item.title: ‘I’m not political’: Tim Cook says his 24-karat gift to Trump wasn’t a political statement, but that Apple is a ‘proud American company’
item.description: Apple CEO Tim Cook set the record straight on his relationship with the Trump administration — and put to rest questions about his future at the company.

item.title: Happiness ranking 2026: What unhappy people have in common as English-speaking countries are shut out of the top 10
item.description: The annual World Happiness Report analyzes every nation’s life satisfaction. Why is the United States so far down? Happiness may be hard to quantify, but for the data-obsessed, the World Happiness Report is as close as you can get.

item.title: ‘Ready or Not 2’ Offers Gory Fun But Doesn’t Quite Level Up
item.description: Samara Weaving gets hunted by a whole new set of creepy elites in Matt Bettinelli-Olpin and Tyler Gillett’s ‘Ready or Not 2: Here I Come.’

item.title: Samsung Galaxy S24 FE, Shokz OpenFit 2 Plus et forfait NRJ Mobile : les 3 meilleurs bons plans Tech du jeudi 19 mars 2026
item.description: Chaque jour, Frandroid scrute le web à la recherche des meilleures réductions sur les produits high-tech.

item.title: Meta rolls out new AI content enforcement systems while reducing reliance on third-party vendors
item.description: Meta believes these AI systems can detect more violations with greater accuracy, better prevent scams, respond more quickly to real-world events, and reduce over-enforcement.

item.title: HBO Max renews Neighbors for season 2 — and I can't wait to watch more of the 'hilariously absurd' documentary
item.description: Neighbors is a very binge worthy documentary series, and it's just been confirmed that we're getting even more.

item.title: The complete Stranger Things DVD set includes 25 discs and costs around $200
item.description: Stranger Things fans will soon have permanent access to the Upside Down, as a full DVD set is now available to preorder. Stranger Things: The Complete Series comes in Blu-Ray and 4K UHD editions. This collection includes all five seasons of the hit show, which totals 25 discs. It'll be available at brick-and-mortar and online retailers beginning on July 28. Prices range from $200 to $260, depending on the media type and edition. Return to Hawkins this July with STRANGER THINGS: THE COMPLETE SERIES, available in Special and Deluxe Editions on Blu-ray and 4K UHD. Pre-order in the US via Arrow: ...

item.title: Macy’s store closures update: Doomed locations will shutter over a longer timeline than previously planned
item.description: The retailer’s ongoing plan to close roughly 150 stores will now continue through 2028. ‘We can be flexible,’ CFO Tom Edwards said this week. Your local Macy’s might not be closing its doors as soon as previously expected.

item.title: Meta’s latest smart glasses feature would have been perfect at the Winter Olympics — but I’m frustrated it can’t be used by everyone
item.description: Meta’s smart glasses are getting their v23 update right now — and the standout upgrade would have been perfect for the Winter Olympics.

item.title: Ubisoft continues with its cost-cutting program as it cuts 100+ jobs and ends game development at its Tom Clancy studio Red Storm Entertainment
item.description: Ubisoft has reportedly ended game development at its Tom Clancy studio, Red Storm Entertainment, and laid off more than 100 staff members.

item.title: Problems with your Android VPN? Proton VPN warns it's Google's fault
item.description: If your Android VPN suddenly stopped working, you're not alone. Proton VPN has called out Google over a seven-month-old Android 16 bug that breaks internet connections after an app update, leaving users exposed and frustrated.

item.title: Ubisoft ends development at Tom Clancy studio Red Storm
item.description: Ubisoft is ceasing game development at its studio, Red Storm Entertainment, best known for its work on the Tom Clancy’s series. While the studio is set to remain open, 105 people will be laid off, a Ubisoft source told GamesIndustry.biz. Those who survive the cull will reportedly continue to work on the Snowdrop engine, used in many of Ubisoft’s tentpole games over the last decade, including Star Wars Outlaws and Avatar: Frontiers of Pandora more recently. Red Storm had been working on an untitled Splinter Cell VR game that was canceled in 2022, as well as the also-canceled The Division Heartl...

item.title: I saw Loewe's new small 4K TVs with high-end image quality and sound, and they look very tempting for cineasts in a small space
item.description: Small and mighty when it comes to both sound and pictures

item.title: The best laptop deals live ahead of Amazons Big Spring Sale: Its a MacBook show so far
item.description: The best laptop deals live ahead of Amazon's Big Spring Sale include discounts on M4 MacBooks and an Asus gaming convertible.

item.title: Nvidia's Earth-2 models, including 'climate in a bottle', want to change weather forecasting for everyone across the world
item.description: We hear how Nvidia's Earth-2 weather models could revolutionize the future of forecasting.
